examples.text:
10th century, Al-Mutanabbi
إِذَا أَنْتَ أَكْرَمْتَ الْكَرِيمَ مَلَكْتَهُ / وَإِنْ أَنْتَ أَكْرَمْتَ اللَّئِيمَ تَمَرَّدَا
ʔiḏā ʔanta ʔakramta al-karīma malaktahu / waʔin ʔanta ʔakramta l-laʔīma tamarradā
examples.english: (please add an English translation of this quotation)

examples.text: كَرِيمٌ هُوَ الَّذِي يَسْتَمِعُ إِلَى الآخَرِينَ وَيَسَاعِدُهُمْ بِكُلِّ مَا يَحْتَاجُونَ إِلَيْهِ.
examples.english: He is generous, as he listens to others and helps them with everything they need.

senses.gloss: a title of God in Islam
a male given name, Karim or Kareem

etymology.text:
فَعِيل (faʕīl)-type stative-verb derived from the active participle from the verb كَرُمَ (karuma, “to become noble, to become generous”).

Borrowed from French crème (“cream”).
